methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Albacore is a seed-stage company in the Ocean & Maritime sector founded by John Huddleston and Dante Vaisbort. The company has raised $6.5M to date. The comparable universe consists of five private peers operating in adjacent ocean technology and maritime solutions: Standard Subsea, Clippership, NetworkOcean, Thalassa Robotics, and Odin Dynamics. No direct public comparables exist in this space.

The stage-progression method is applicable here, using the $6.5M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $16M. Given limited methodological diversity with only one applicable approach, the triangulation range extends from $14M (low) to $19M (median), with $16M as the median point.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $14M to $19M, with $16M as the central estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of public market benchmarks and reliance on a single valuation method. The private comparables provide contextual grounding but limited direct pricing signals. Future rounds will benefit from revenue traction, clearer unit economics, or additional comparable transactions within the ocean-tech ecosystem to narrow this range materially.
